methods.description: This method describes the modeling of mineral dust emissions and their transport in the atmosphere using the ECHAM6-HAM2 model. It focuses on the interaction of dust with solar radiation and cloud structures, and the conditions under which dust is emitted from various sources.

steps.description: Use the ECHAM6-HAM2 model at a resolution of T63L31 to simulate aerosol interactions.
steps.searchable_summary: initialize ECHAM6-HAM2 model with T63L31 resolution

steps.description: Focus on mineral dust and its impact on solar radiation and cloud structure.
steps.searchable_summary: simulate mineral dust impact on solar radiation and clouds

steps.description: Implement dust emission from bare soils and sparse vegetation areas when surface wind velocity exceeds a critical threshold.
steps.searchable_summary: model dust emission based on wind velocity and soil conditions

steps.description: Use the dust emission scheme based on saltation bombardment and parameterize horizontal dust flux as a cubic function of surface wind velocity.
steps.searchable_summary: apply saltation bombardment scheme for dust emission

steps.description: Transport dust via tracers in the atmosphere component ECHAM and model deposition processes.
steps.searchable_summary: simulate dust transport and deposition in ECHAM

methods.description: This method involves the inclusion of a dynamic soil albedo scheme in the model, parameterized based on plant net primary productivity (NPP).

steps.description: Include a dynamic soil albedo scheme in the model, parameterized based on plant net primary productivity (NPP).
steps.searchable_summary: implement dynamic soil albedo scheme based on NPP

steps.description: Evaluate the soil albedo separately for the visible and near-infrared range.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate soil albedo for visible and near-infrared range
